Home.title: Operate nodes, servers, and access in one control surface.
Home.subtitle: Vestri keeps your infrastructure organized with clear permissions, deployment visibility, and secure user flows.

cta.preview: Sign in to open your dashboard and manage nodes, templates, and active game servers.

errors.invalidSession: Your session has expired or is invalid. Please log in again.

hero.title: Set up and run your game server platform with confidence.
hero.subtitle: This guide walks you through worker onboarding, server lifecycle operations, and security controls with practical defaults.

chapters.title: Chapters
chapters.nodeOnboardingDescription: Prepare worker defaults, trust chain, and node credentials before provisioning servers.
chapters.serverLifecycleDescription: Create, operate, and update servers in a repeatable flow.
chapters.accessSecurityDescription: Control permissions and protect critical operations with step-up checks.

prerequisites.title: Before You Start
prerequisites.description: Prepare these basics once so the complete setup flow is smooth.

items.1: A Vestri account with access to your workspace
items.2: A reachable worker endpoint and API key for node registration
items.3: A template strategy for your game servers (versions and defaults)

configReference.title: Configuration Reference
configReference.description: Complete reference of environment variables and worker settings options currently used by the stack.

environment.title: Environment variables
environment.description: Frontend, backend, migration, and bundled compose infrastructure variables.

workerSettings.title: Worker settings.json options
workerSettings.description: All available keys from /etc/vestri/settings.json with current defaults and runtime behavior.

configReference.parsingHint: Backend bool env parsing accepts: 1, true, yes. List env values are comma-separated.

bootstrap.title: Config and trust bootstrap
bootstrap.description: Before creating your first node, do these lightweight checks. Keep it simple and use defaults first.

steps.1: Start the worker once so default files are created automatically.
steps.2: If TLS is enabled in worker settings, copy the worker CA certificate to backend trust.
steps.3: Use the worker API key when creating the node in Vestri.
steps.4: Match the node URL scheme: https with TLS, explicit http only for intentional HTTP mode.

rules.1: If TLS is enabled on the worker, copy CA cert from worker to backend trust (default: /etc/vestri/certs/ca.crt -> ./certs/worker-cas/node-name.crt).
rules.2: If TLS is enabled, use https in node host/base URL (default worker URL style: https://worker-host:8031).
rules.3: Only use http:// if TLS is intentionally disabled on the worker.
rules.4: When adding the node in UI, use API key from the worker key file (default: /etc/vestri/api.key).

bootstrap.screenshotLabel: Settings, API key, and CA trust placement

systemd.title: Start services with systemd
systemd.description: For stable production startup, run the Docker stack and worker as systemd services.

steps.1: Create one service for the Docker Compose stack (frontend/backend/postgres/redis).
steps.2: Create one service for the standalone worker process.
steps.3: Reload systemd and enable both services.
steps.4: Verify status after reboot with systemctl status.

connectNode.title: Connect Your First Node
connectNode.description: Nodes are the execution layer. Register at least one worker node before creating servers.

steps.1: Open Nodes and create a new worker entry.
steps.2: Enter host/base URL and API key for the worker.
steps.3: Save and run a health check to verify connectivity.
steps.4: Confirm your role and permissions on that node.

createServer.title: Create and Provision a Server
createServer.description: Provision servers from templates to keep deployments consistent and predictable.

steps.1: Open Dashboard and select the target node.
steps.2: Choose a template and set identifier and version fields.
steps.3: Confirm required agreements and create the server.
steps.4: Open the workspace and verify status + startup.

operateServer.title: Operate During Runtime
operateServer.description: Use one workspace for lifecycle actions, logs, console sessions, files, and configs.

steps.1: Use Start/Stop and status refresh to control the stack.
steps.2: Watch logs with auto-update and manual refresh fallback.
steps.3: Use the file browser/editor for config and content updates.
steps.4: Validate changes in console before and after restart.

teamAndAccess.title: Manage Team Access Safely
teamAndAccess.description: Invite users with explicit roles and keep sensitive actions protected by step-up verification.

steps.2: Use invite flows and remove stale guests regularly.
steps.3: Require step-up checks for critical actions (delete, passkey changes).
steps.4: Review active sessions and rotate credentials when needed.

updateRoutine.title: Build a Clean Update Routine
updateRoutine.description: Treat image updates as an explicit maintenance action to reduce operational surprises.

steps.1: Check indicators for outdated server images.
steps.2: Repull images intentionally during maintenance windows.
steps.3: Restart and validate service health and logs.

nextSteps.title: Next Steps
nextSteps.description: Start with one node and one template, then expand to a repeatable deployment workflow.
